refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and workflows to improve energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors integrated with telemetry for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to regulate power usage based on load and terrain.
DP3: Use data analytics and machine learning models to optimize operational parameters and scheduling.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring and control modules.
Feedback loops between control and optimization algorithms.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via well-defined telemetry and tasking interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting real-time responsiveness.
Optimization models require sufficient historical data for accuracy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing optimization separately to reduce complexity and improve maintainability.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical vehicle systems including sensors and control actuators responsible for energy monitoring and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends energy consumption and load data to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives control commands from NGHE Solution or Teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of sensing and control functions; low diffusion as control logic is centralized.
Contains optimization and data analytics; moderate diffusion due to cross-module data sharing.
Limited containment; mainly remote control interface, some diffusion risks if control logic duplicated.
Minimal relevance to energy efficiency functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy monitoring and control are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and latency.
containment_risks: Potential overloading of Vehicle module with complex control and sensing logic.
Dependency on reliable network link for telemetry and tasking may introduce latency or data loss.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting optimization functions into NGHE Solution module to reduce Vehicle complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk in optimization data sharing between Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor Vehicle for monitoring and control; optimization may require separate containment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data validation and energy efficiency KPI measurement.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of monitoring and control to Vehicle; optimization allocation to NGHE Solution needs confirmation.

recommended_revisions: Consider modularizing optimization functions into NGHE Solution to reduce Vehicle complexity and diffusion.
Clarify interface definitions for optimization data exchange.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are mostly sound, optimization functions require clearer modular separation to reduce diffusion and complexity risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and workflows to improve energy efficiency.

revise.why: To separate optimization functions from Vehicle module and assign them clearly to NGHE Solution module, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.

assumptions: Reliable sensor data and network connectivity are available.
Control algorithms can operate within Vehicle module constraints.
open_risks: Potential latency or data loss on network link affecting telemetry and control.
Complexity of optimization algorithms impacting system performance.
evidence_needed: Performance benchmarks of control algorithms within Vehicle module.
Data flow analysis between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for optimization.
